slides.title: AI-Native Enterprise Mini MBA
slides.subtitle: Business Foundations, Leadership & Enterprise Transformation in the AI Era
slides.body: Welcome to the AI-Native Enterprise Mini MBA. Throughout this programme you will step into the role of CEO of JourneyX, a digital travel platform, and make strategic, financial, operational and transformation decisions. You will learn timeless business foundations, explore how enterprises operate today, and discover how organisations must evolve their execution models in the AI era.
slides.facilitator_notes: Welcome everyone. This is not an AI course. This is not a technology course. This is a modern business leadership programme. The objective is to help leaders understand how businesses work and how enterprises must evolve in the AI era. Throughout the programme participants will act as CEO of JourneyX and make decisions that impact Financial Health, Customer Health, People Health, Operational Health, Innovation Health and Risk & Governance Health.

slides.title: Why This Programme Exists
slides.body: Throughout my career I have worked across strategy, sales, operations, project management, capital investment, governance and transformation. One thing became clear: many talented leaders are experts in their own field but struggle to understand how the rest of the business works. Finance leaders often don't understand technology. Technology leaders often don't understand finance. Project leaders don't always understand strategy. Many managers have never been taught how businesses actually work as an integrated system. This programme was created to bridge those gaps and help leaders understand both the foundations of business and how enterprises must evolve in the AI era.
slides.facilitator_notes: Tell your story rather than reading the slide. Give 2-3 examples from your own experience where teams made poor decisions because they lacked understanding of another business function. Reinforce that this course is designed to create well-rounded leaders, not specialists. Conclude with: 'The best leaders understand how the whole business works, not just their own department.'

slides.title: Selected Experiences
slides.body: Throughout my career I have had opportunities to learn from industry leaders, participate in conferences, contribute to transformation initiatives and build relationships with people from different industries and backgrounds. Here are some memorable moments from that journey.
slides.facilitator_notes: Spend 5-7 minutes introducing the photos and the stories behind them. One of the photos includes Lewis Pugh, whose story of adaptation and resilience will reappear later in the course. Do not reveal which photos are real and which are AI-generated. Build curiosity and confidence. Before moving to the next slide ask participants: 'How many of you believe you could confidently identify which of these photos are real and which are AI-generated?'

slides.title: AI-Era Leadership Lesson
slides.quote: Trust, but Verify. Seeing Is No Longer Believing. Critical thinking becomes a leadership capability.
slides.facilitator_notes: Reveal that Photo 4 was AI-generated. Ask participants how many selected Photo 4 correctly. Then ask: What clues did you use? What made some photos appear more authentic than others? Explain that AI can now generate highly convincing images, reports, presentations, analyses and recommendations. The lesson is not to distrust everything. The lesson is to develop critical thinking and verification habits. Leaders must increasingly make decisions in environments where content can be generated instantly and convincingly. In the AI era, trust remains important, but verification becomes essential.

slides.title: Critical Thinking in the AI Era
points: Question information before accepting it as fact.
Verify evidence and data sources before making decisions.
Use AI as an advisor, not as an unquestioned authority.
Focus on judgment, context and decision quality, not just information.

Example: If AI says customer churn is increasing because of pricing, don't immediately reduce prices. Ask: What data supports this? What assumptions were made? Could there be other causes? Verify before you decide.
slides.facilitator_notes: Spend 5-7 minutes on this slide. Explain that critical thinking does not mean being sceptical of everything. It means evaluating information, verifying evidence, challenging assumptions and making sound judgments before taking action. In the past, creating reports, presentations and analyses required significant effort. Today AI can generate them in seconds. The leadership challenge is no longer obtaining information; it is determining whether the information is accurate, complete, unbiased and relevant. Use the photo activity as the example. Participants saw convincing content and many were confident in their answers. Then use the customer churn example: imagine an AI assistant tells a CEO that customer churn is increasing because prices are too high. A leader with poor critical thinking immediately reduces prices. A leader with strong critical thinking asks: What evidence supports this conclusion? How reliable is the data? What other explanations exist? Is the issue pricing, service quality, competitors, product features or customer experience? Reinforce the principle: Trust, but Verify. Seeing Is No Longer Believing.

slides.title: Adapting to a Changing World
slides.description: Lewis Pugh — How I Swam the North Pole
slides.facilitator_notes: Before playing the video, tell participants that one of the photos they saw earlier was a real photo with Lewis Pugh. Briefly explain that Lewis became the first person to complete a long-distance swim at the North Pole. Ask participants to think about what happens when the environment changes but people, organisations and leaders continue operating the same way. After the video, facilitate a discussion around adaptation, resilience, leadership and transformation. Emphasise that successful organisations do not evolve because technology exists; they evolve because the environment around them changes. Connect this to the course philosophy: Foundations Remain. Execution Evolves. The world changes first. Leaders and organisations must adapt second.

activities.title: Real or AI?
activities.question: Looking at the five photos in the collage, which photo do you believe is AI-generated?

rewards.name: Cafe voucher
rewards.description: $20 at a café of your choice
